core_model.structure_summary: A scalar discrete-time map where the next value is a convex combination of two sigmoidal responses to a shifted and oppositely-shifted mean field, whose fixed points and cycles are studied as parameters vary.

variables.meaning: scalar fraction / mean field, self-consistently coupled to itself
mixing weight between the two opposite response types
external bias / drive of the sigmoid argument

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A one-dimensional self-consistent map combining two oppositely-signed sigmoidal responses to its own value, characterized by its fixed points and periodic orbits.

notes: Underlying model is an agent-based binary-choice system, but the central analytic object is the mean-field self-consistency map n=F(n) and its iteration n(t+1)=F(n(t)); fixed points, bifurcations, and adoption cycles are the main results. Skeleton is already essentially bare (the deterministic mean-field limit).
